enonce.texte: Identify the modal verb in each sentence and choose its correct meaning from the options provided.

questions.texte: Read each sentence and identify the modal verb.

sousQuestions.texte: She can speak three languages fluently.
You must wear a helmet when riding a motorbike.
May I borrow your pen, please?
You should eat more fruits and vegetables.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct modal verb from the box. Some modals may be used more than once.

Modals: can, could, must, have to, should, may, might, needn't

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the appropriate modal verb.

sousQuestions.texte: She ______ play the piano very well. She's been taking lessons for years.
You ______ come to the meeting if you're too busy. It's not mandatory.
We ______ respect our elders.
It ______ rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella just in case.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct form of 'must' or 'have to'. Remember: 'must' expresses personal/internal obligation, while 'have to' expresses external obligation.

sousQuestions.texte: I ______ finish this project by tomorrow. The boss is expecting it.
sousQuestions.reponse: have to (external obligation imposed by the boss)

sousQuestions.texte: I ______ stop eating so much sugar. It's bad for my health.
In Cameroon, you ______ have a driver's license to drive a car.
I really ______ call my grandmother. I promised I would.

intitule: Giving Advice with Should and Ought To

enonce.texte: Read each situation and give advice using 'should', 'shouldn't', 'ought to', or 'ought not to'.

sousQuestions.texte: Your friend has been feeling very tired lately and lacks energy.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should/ought to see a doctor. You should/ought to get more rest and eat a balanced diet.

sousQuestions.texte: Your brother is spending too much time on social media and neglecting his studies.
sousQuestions.reponse: You shouldn't spend so much time on social media. You should/ought to focus more on your studies.

sousQuestions.texte: You want to improve your English speaking skills.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should/ought to practice speaking every day. You could also join an English club or watch movies in English.

sousQuestions.texte: A friend wants to travel to an unfamiliar area at night.
sousQuestions.reponse: You shouldn't travel there alone at night. You should/ought to travel with someone or go during the daytime.

intitule: Probability and Possibility with May, Might, Could

enonce.texte: Express probability or possibility in the following situations. Use 'may', 'might', or 'could'. Sometimes more than one answer is possible.

questions.texte: Express the level of probability in each situation.

sousQuestions.texte: There are dark clouds in the sky. (It / rain)
sousQuestions.reponse: It might/may/could rain. (possibility based on evidence)

sousQuestions.texte: Someone is knocking at the door. (It / be the postman)
My phone is ringing, but I don't recognize the number. (It / be my mother)
The ground is wet and there are puddles everywhere. (It / rain during the night)
sousQuestions.reponse: It must have rained during the night. (certainty based on evidence)

enonce.texte: Add the correct question tag to each sentence that contains a modal verb. Follow the rule: positive statement → negative tag, negative statement → positive tag.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct question tag.

sousQuestions.texte: She can speak Chinese, ______?
We must leave now, ______?
They shouldn't be late, ______?
You could help me with this, ______?

enonce.texte: Read the following scenarios and write appropriate responses using modal verbs. Use a variety of modals to express ability, permission, obligation, advice, and probability.

questions.texte: Respond to each scenario using the appropriate modal verb.

sousQuestions.texte: A tourist in Cameroon wants to know if they can take photos at a traditional ceremony.
sousQuestions.reponse: You may take photos, but you should ask for permission first. You must respect the traditional customs.

sousQuestions.texte: A student is worried about an upcoming exam and asks for advice.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should start revising early. You ought to study every day. You mustn't wait until the last minute.

sousQuestions.texte: A friend is considering buying a used car but is unsure about the decision.
sousQuestions.reponse: You could take a mechanic to inspect it. You might find a better deal elsewhere. You should check the car's history before buying.

sousQuestions.texte: A student wants to attend a school trip but hasn't received permission yet.
sousQuestions.reponse: You can ask your parents for permission. You may need to submit a signed permission slip. You might have to speak to the principal directly.

enonce.texte: Complete the paragraph below with the correct modal verbs. Use a variety of modals including can, could, must, have to, should, may, might, needn't.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the appropriate modal verbs.

sousQuestions.texte: In many communities, young people ______ (1) participate in volunteer activities. They ______ (2) choose to work with NGOs or humanitarian organizations like the Red Cross. While volunteering, they ______ (3) learn new skills and develop a sense of responsibility. However, volunteers ______ (4) follow the rules and regulations of the organization. They ______ (5) respect the community members they are helping. Volunteers ______ (6) also take care of their own safety. They ______ (7) work in dangerous areas without proper supervision. Finally, volunteers ______ (8) remember that their work can make a real difference.
sousQuestions.reponse:
1. can/may (ability/possibility)

2. may/can (possibility/ability)

3. can (ability)

4. must/have to (obligation)

5. must/should (obligation/advice)

6. must/should (advice/obligation)

7. mustn't/shouldn't (prohibition/advice)

8. should (advice)
